query: You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository. The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 441a9c92). ## Issue to Resolve Bug on parameter's class that reached maximum line ## Expected Behavior No error. ## Observed Behavior Error. ## Steps to Reproduce I have parameter lambda code like this, let say my parameter is reached maximum limit of line : There is error detected on `open bracket (`. I already clicked `Format with ktlint`, but still error. ## Your Environment * Version of ktlint used: 0.43.2 * Version of Gradle used (if applicable): AGP 7 * Operating System and version: MacOS Big Sur ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Next time, please post your code instead of an image. It is more convenient when trying to reproduce a problem. The code below is comparable to your example: ``` // Assume, the line breaks at the pipe symbol on the right -> | val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null ``` The `ktlint --verbose --relative` command results in: ``` src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:1: Exceeded max line length (80) (cannot be auto-corrected) (max-line-length) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:13: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:25: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:37: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:49: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:61: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:73: Parameter should be on a separate line (unless all parameters can fit a single line) (parameter-list-wrapping) src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:1:83: Missing newline before ")" (parameter-list-wrapping) ``` From this it can be seen that both the `max-line-length` and the `parameter-list-wrapping` report violations in the original code. Auto-correct by the `parameter-list-wrapping` resolves the max line length violation and results in code below: ``` // Assume, next line exceeds the max line length -> | val foo1: ( ( s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String ) -> Unit )? = null ``` but that same rule immediately reports now another lint error as you reported as well: ``` src/main/kotlin/Issue1324.kt:3:5: Unnecessary newline before "(" (parameter-list-wrapping) ``` So far, I have found no way to refactor the code manually in such a way that the code is accepted by both *ktlint* as well as by the IntelliJ default code formatting which nowadays is a requirement when we make (bugfixes on) rules. For example, the previous code sample will be transformed by IntelliJ default formatting to following: ``` // Assume, next line exceeds the max line length -> | val foo1: ( ( s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String ) -> Unit )? = null ``` This is horrible code. For now, I see no other way for you to disable both rules and stick with one really long line: ``` val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null // ktlint-disable max-line-length parameter-list-wrapping ``` Maybe, someone else has a briljant idea how to the resulting code should look like in such a way that both ktlint as well as IntelliJ default formatting do not alter that code. Next step would then be to fix the rules do that the code will be formatted as such. ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `CHANGELOG.md` - `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/ParameterListWrappingRule.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality ## Important Evaluation Notes **Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing. **If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files ## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS **NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

**Adjusting formatting logic**

I realize that ktlint format applies all rules with autocorrect, but it might have skipped wrapping the parameter list due to how wrapNullableType interacts with function types. To fix this, I should adjust wrapNullableType to avoid inserting a newline after the outer left parenthesis when it’s followed by the function type starting with VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST since that newline is unnecessary. Ultimately, it would be better to keep `((` on the same line. So, instead of a newline, I might just use whitespace or nothing to keep lines tidy.**Refining parameter formatting**

I think the rule shouldn't emit warnings like "Parameter of nullable type should be on a separate line" for the outer left parenthesis. Maybe it makes more sense for the FUNCTION_TYPE instead. It’s possible that the original intention was to wrap the nullable type differently. The goal of wrapNullableType should produce code like `val foo1: ((...)->Unit)?`, but that introduces a newline in a place that might not be desirable. I realize this is causing errors at the open bracket, indicating a need for a rule adjustment to prevent self-violations in formatting.**Adjusting wrapNullableType behavior**

I think when wrapNullableType is at work, it shouldn't create a newline before the inner left parenthesis when skipping parameter list wrapping. It seems simplest to avoid inserting a newline after the outer left parenthesis if the next element is also a left parenthesis. But right now, it always adds that newline. If we allow wrapping the parameter list for function types, it could lead to a more stable format. Still, there's a concern about the outer right parenthesis newline that parameter list wrapping might not handle. This feels like a potential contradiction in the wrapNullableType rules.**Refining wrapNullableType logic**

I see that wrapNullableType only activates when a nullable type exceeds the maximum line length, particularly when parameters are on a single line. If wrapping the parameter list breaks this up, the nullable type might no longer exceed that length and thus not require the newline. I’m thinking that the order of traversal matters, as NULLABLE_TYPE could be checked before the inner parameter list is processed. In autocorrect mode, inserting a newline after the outer left parenthesis could be problematic because it might be removed during parameter wrapping. To stabilize the final formatting, perhaps it’s best to eliminate the exception that causes the parameter list not to wrap in certain functions.**Examining wrapNullableType behavior**

It seems that enabling wrapping for inner parameters would solve the newline issue created by wrapNullableType before the inner left parenthesis, which gets deleted later. However, this could lead to wrapping parameters in nullable function types where it hadn't occurred before, which might be necessary to fix max line length. 

Looking at the ktlint official code style, they likely want to format long parameter lists in nullable function types distinctly, but this could conflict with the rule to avoid unnecessary newlines before parentheses. We need to refine the error checks to prevent unnecessary newline errors in certain cases, especially when wrapping nullable types. By adjusting the parameter list wrapping rules, we can avoid conflicts while ensuring stable and tidy formatting.**Revising wrapNullableType implementation**

To achieve better stability in formatting, I’m thinking about allowing wrapParameterList to handle wrapping for inner parameters, which might help avoid unnecessary newlines created by wrapNullableType. The goal here is to ensure that when we format a nullable function type, it aligns more closely with IntelliJ's standards, particularly in how parentheses and parameters are handled. 

I believe the optimal approach would be to remove or adjust the wrapNullableType to not insert newlines unnecessarily, especially between outer and inner parentheses. This would mean focusing on wrapping inner parameter lists effectively while keeping the nullable type structure intact, ultimately simplifying the code and addressing conflicts with IntelliJ’s formatting.**Evaluating wrapNullableType function**

I'm considering that wrapNullableType gets invoked for NULLABLE_TYPE and can emit errors, especially with autocorrect enabled. If I alter this function, my priority is to avoid reporting violations after formatting. It seems wise to drop its emissions in situations where parameter list wrapping can resolve max line length issues.

I think wrapNullableType should check if child FUNCTION_TYPE has a VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST exceeding the limit. If it does, it shouldn't emit or wrap. I'll also check for relevant tests in the ktlint-ruleset-standard module to ensure my changes align with existing behavior.

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.KtLintAssertThat.Companion.assertThatRule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.test.LintViolation
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test

class Issue1324NullableFunctionTypeWrappingTest {
    private val parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat =
        assertThatRule(
            provider = { ParameterListWrappingRule() },
            additionalRuleProviders =
                setOf(
                    // Apply the IndentationRule always as additional rule, so that the formattedCode in the unit test looks
                    // correct.
                    RuleProvider { IndentationRule() },
                ),
        )

    @Test
    fun `Given a nullable function type which exceeds max line length due to its parameter list then wrap the parameter list`() {
        val code =
            """
            val foo1: ((s1: String, s2: String, s3: String, s4: String, s5: String, s6: String) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()
        val formattedCode =
            """
            val foo1: ((
                s1: String,
                s2: String,
                s3: String,
                s4: String,
                s5: String,
                s6: String
            ) -> Unit)? = null
            """.trimIndent()

        parameterListWrappingRuleAssertThat(code)
            .withEditorConfigOverride(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY to 80)
            .hasLintViolations(
                LintViolation(1, 13, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 25, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 37, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 49, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 61, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 73, "Parameter should start on a newline"),
                LintViolation(1, 83, "Missing newline before \")\""),
            ).isFormattedAs(formattedCode)
    }
}
"}

**Evaluating lint violations**

I’m spotting actual lint violations, specifically around parameter list wrapping for inner parameters and missing newlines before certain parentheses. In a previous test, we only expected `wrapNullableType`. Then with `skip` removed, `needToWrapParameterList` becomes true due to the long line caused by `var changesListener:`. After the change, `exceedsMaxLineLength()` evaluates as true, but for issue 1255, we want to wrap the nullable type instead of the entire parameter list if it fits on one line.**Clarifying lint behavior**

In the current flow, `wrapNullableType` processes NULLABLE_TYPE and wraps outer parentheses, but when we're linting the original code without autocorrect, we just report violations without making changes. The `hasLintViolations` function checks for lint errors that can be auto-corrected, so I'm considering if it runs with autoCorrect enabled. It seems like `.hasLintViolations` might lint with that in mind. While parameter list violations are correct, I want to ensure that we don't wrap the parameter list in a nullable function type if it fits on a new line. We've removed the skip entirely.**Establishing conditional wrapping**

I realize we need a condition to skip wrapping the parameter list within a nullable function type if the nullable type itself is wrapped. To handle this in `needToWrapParameterList`, I could reintroduce the function `isFunctionTypeWrappedInNullableType()`, but it would depend on whether the function type fits when wrapped. Alternatively, in `needToWrapParameterList`, I can check if the parameter list is in a function type wrapped in a nullable type and if wrapping it keeps it under max line length. However, for issue 1324, we do need to wrap the parameter list, so we’ll need a helper function to compute this correctly.**Refining parameter list wrapping**

I can apply the same logic in `needToWrapParameterList`: if the parameter list is part of a function type wrapped in a nullable type and fits on a single line when wrapped, I'll return false, preventing the parameter list from wrapping. However, after the `wrapNullableType` autocorrect inserts a newline, the parameter list may not exceed max length, which can complicate things. In lint-only mode, we still don’t want to report that the parameter should start on a newline. To address this, I'll create a new function called `isFunctionTypeInNullableTypeThatCanBeWrappedToSatisfyMaxLineLength()` to help determine if wrapping should apply.**Implementing nullability logic**

I need to set the same preconditions for `wrapNullableType` in `needToWrapParameterList`: if the node (nullable type) exceeds max length, has a function type, and contains a non-empty, single-line parameter list, then the parameter list should not wrap. So, I'm implementing a function to check if it's part of a nullable function type that should be wrapped. I'll need this check before verifying if `textContains('\n')`. 

This means I can create an extension on `VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST` to help with this logic. The conditions will ensure we’re correctly handling cases with parameter lists and maintain necessary functionality from `wrapNullableType`. We’ll also need to integrate the new condition into `needToWrapParameterList` to ensure the right checks are made for wrapping. Let's go ahead and edit the code accordingly.

content.text:
Implemented a fix in `ParameterListWrappingRule` for nullable function types whose *function type itself* still exceeds `max_line_length`:

- `wrapNullableType` now only wraps the nullable type `(` … `)?` when the inner `FUNCTION_TYPE` fits on a single line after that wrap.
- Otherwise, ktlint will wrap the inner function type’s `VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST` (so formatting resolves the long line without producing a follow-up “Unnecessary newline before '('” violation).
- Added a new regression test `Issue1324NullableFunctionTypeWrappingTest` covering the reported scenario.

All `:ktlint-ruleset-standard:test` tests pass.
